Preschool Grants, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–630, title I, § 110102 Stat. 3303Pub. L. 101–476, title IX, § 901(a)(3)104 Stat. 1142Nov. 7, 1988Oct. 30, 1990“The provisions of section 300.300(b)(3) of title 34, Code of Federal Regulations, shall not apply with respect to children aged 3 through 5, inclusive, in any State for any fiscal year for which the State receives a grant under section 619(a)(1) of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [].”20 U.S.C. 1419(a)(1)

children.text: to children with disabilities aged 3 through 5, inclusive; and
at the State’s discretion, to 2-year-old children with disabilities who will turn 3 during the school year.
is eligible under to receive a grant under this subchapter; andsection 1412 of this title
makes a free appropriate public education available to all children with disabilities, aged 3 through 5, residing in the State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate the amount made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year among the States in accordance with paragraph (2) or (3), as the case may be.

children.label: I
children.text: allocate to each State the amount the State received under this section for fiscal year 1997;

children.label: II
children.text: allocate 85 percent of any remaining funds to States on the basis of the States’ relative populations of children aged 3 through 5; and

children.label: III
children.text: allocate 15 percent of those remaining funds to States on the basis of the States’ relative populations of all children aged 3 through 5 who are living in poverty.

children.heading: Data
children.label: ii Data
children.text: For the purpose of making grants under this paragraph, the Secretary shall use the most recent population data, including data on children living in poverty, that are available and satisfactory to the Secretary.

children.heading: Preceding years
children.label: i Preceding years
children.text: No State’s allocation shall be less than its allocation under this section for the preceding fiscal year.

children.label: aa
children.text: the amount the State received under this section for fiscal year 1997; and

children.label: bb
children.text: ⅓ of 1 percent of the amount by which the amount appropriated under subsection (j) for the fiscal year exceeds the amount appropriated for this section for fiscal year 1997;

children.label: aa
children.text: the amount the State received under this section for the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: bb
children.text: that amount multiplied by the percentage by which the increase in the funds appropriated under this section from the preceding fiscal year exceeds 1.5 percent; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the amount the State received under this section for the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: bb
children.text: that amount multiplied by 90 percent of the percentage increase in the amount appropriated under this section from the preceding fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount the State received under this section for the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: that amount multiplied by the sum of 1.5 percent and the percentage increase in the amount appropriated under this section from the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Ratable reductions
children.label: C Ratable reductions
children.text: If the amount available for allocations under this paragraph is insufficient to pay those allocations in full, those allocations shall be ratably reduced, subject to subparagraph (B)(i).

children.label: i
children.text: the amount the State received under this section for fiscal year 1997; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount that bears the same relation to any remaining funds as the increase the State received under this section for the preceding fiscal year over fiscal year 1997 bears to the total of all such increases for all States.

children.heading: Ratable reductions
children.label: B Ratable reductions
children.text: If the amount available for allocations is equal to or less than the amount allocated to the States for fiscal year 1997, each State shall be allocated the amount the State received for fiscal year 1997, ratably reduced, if necessary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State may reserve not more than the amount described in paragraph (2) for administration and other State-level activities in accordance with subsections (e) and (f).

children.label: A
children.text: the percentage increase, if any, from the preceding fiscal year in the State’s allocation under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: the percentage increase, if any, from the preceding fiscal year in the Consumer Price Index For All Urban Consumers published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of administering this section (including the coordination of activities under this subchapter with, and providing technical assistance to, other programs that provide services to children with disabilities) a State may use not more than 20 percent of the maximum amount the State may reserve under subsection (d) for any fiscal year.

children.heading: Administration of subchapter III
children.label: 2 Administration of subchapter III
children.text: Funds described in paragraph (1) may also be used for the administration of subchapter III.

for support services (including establishing and implementing the mediation process required by ), which may benefit children with disabilities younger than 3 or older than 5 as long as those services also benefit children with disabilities aged 3 through 5;section 1415(e) of this title
for direct services for children eligible for services under this section;
for activities at the State and local levels to meet the performance goals established by the State under ;section 1412(a)(15) of this title

to supplement other funds used to develop and implement a statewide coordinated services system designed to improve results for children and families, including children with disabilities and their families, but not more than 1 percent of the amount received by the State under this section for a fiscal year;

to provide early intervention services (which shall include an educational component that promotes school readiness and incorporates preliteracy, language, and numeracy skills) in accordance with subchapter III to children with disabilities who are eligible for services under this section and who previously received services under subchapter III until such children enter, or are eligible under State law to enter, kindergarten; or
at the State’s discretion, to continue service coordination or case management for families who receive services under subchapter III.

children.heading: Subgrants to local educational agencies
children.label: g Subgrants to local educational agencies

children.heading: Base payments
children.label: A Base payments
children.text: The State shall first award each local educational agency described in paragraph (1) the amount that agency would have received under this section for fiscal year 1997 if the State had distributed 75 percent of its grant for that year under , as such section was then in effect.section 1419(c)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: allocate 85 percent of any remaining funds to those local educational agencies on the basis of the relative numbers of children enrolled in public and private elementary schools and secondary schools within the local educational agency’s jurisdiction; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allocate 15 percent of those remaining funds to those local educational agencies in accordance with their relative numbers of children living in poverty, as determined by the State educational agency.

children.heading: Reallocation of funds
children.label: 2 Reallocation of funds
children.text: If a State educational agency determines that a local educational agency is adequately providing a free appropriate public education to all children with disabilities aged 3 through 5 residing in the area served by the local educational agency with State and local funds, the State educational agency may reallocate any portion of the funds under this section that are not needed by that local educational agency to provide a free appropriate public education to other local educational agencies in the State that are not adequately providing special education and related services to all children with disabilities aged 3 through 5 residing in the areas the other local educational agencies serve.

children.heading: Subchapter III inapplicable
children.label: h Subchapter III inapplicable
children.text: Subchapter III does not apply to any child with a disability receiving a free appropriate public education, in accordance with this subchapter, with funds received under this section.

children.heading: State defined
children.label: i State defined
children.text: In this section, the term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary.
